figures.caption: Figure 7: The prompt template used to construct the prompt sent to the LLM.
Table 4: Aggregate statistics about the user feedback for Auto-Diagnose.

Figure 4: Test driver and the system under test (SUT) that it controls to exercise business logic in the integration test. Each component produces its own log files, typically split by log level, and named dynamically after the component.

Figure 2: Different kinds of integration tests employed by teams at Google, based on a survey of 239 respondents. Note that a single team can have several types of these integration tests, therefore, the sum exceeds 100%.
Figure 8: Excerpts of feedback on Auto-Diagnose Critique findings from interviews conducted with 11 participants.
Figure 6: The LLM-based diagnosis result posted as a finding to Critique.
Table 2: Aggregate statistics about Auto-Diagnose findings posted to Critique and the properties of the failing integration tests it diagnosed.
